research_basis: Durable workflow practice favors persisted state, pause/replay semantics, activity retries, and explicit event histories for long-running work.
Saga/compensation practice favors explicit rollback, compensation, retry, or non-reversible warning by action semantics.
OpenTelemetry observability guidance supports correlating traces, logs, and events for audit navigation.
NIST AI RMF frames AI risk management as continuous govern, map, measure, and manage activity.
Progressive disclosure keeps first-value product language simple while advanced governance remains available on request.

scenario_results.notes: Mission Control/Ops Inbox surface shows briefs, evidence gaps, missions, tasks, approvals, actions, memory changes, and learning opportunities.
One item visibly progresses through Inbox, Brief, Claim, Action, and Learn.
Product boundary copy explains source systems as systems of record and CornerStone as intelligence/evidence/mission/action-control/learning layer.
Personal memory can be explicitly promoted into organization namespace with provenance, policy, and audit.
First value and basic mission work use plain product language while advanced governance remains optional.
Provider action trace is ConnectorHub-mediated with no direct provider access, secret exposure, raw access, or arbitrary agent code.
Mission autonomy revoke records the event and blocks future autonomous execution outside safe cleanup.
Missing evidence, policy denial, connector failure, model disagreement, unclear goal, and high-risk action escalate with reason, resolution, and minimum human decision.
Mission outcome records evidence, judge assessment, owner acceptance, errors, escalations, and lessons.
After-action review and Autonomy Scorecard include goal, actions, evidence, judge/owner outcome, errors, escalations, lessons, playbooks, and rollback/correction options.
Mission audit export includes timeline events, tool calls, policy decisions, evidence, judge outputs, approvals, and action results.
Autonomy metrics prioritize outcome quality over raw autonomy ratio.
Action reversibility covers rollback, compensation, retry, and explicit non-reversible explanation paths.
UX labels present one CornerStone product and visible capabilities, not internal repository names as the required mental model.

recommended_next_steps: Create a draft claim from this Evidence Bundle if the user wants durable truth work.
Collect more evidence if the decision requires higher confidence.
brief.title: Brief for mission-control-alpha
uncertainty: This brief is grounded only in the attached Evidence Bundle.
Add more sources before using it as broad organizational truth.

authority.blocked_reason: Owner approval is required before shared truth or autonomous action use.

resolution_path: Use an active Mission Goal Contract.

escalation.minimum_required_human_decision: Approve retry, choose a fallback source, or pause the mission.
escalation.reason: Connector capability failed or is unavailable.
escalation.recommended_resolution: Retry through ConnectorHub or quarantine the connector attempt.

escalation.minimum_required_human_decision: Approve, reject, or revise the high-risk action.
escalation.reason: Requested action is high-risk or externally impactful.
escalation.recommended_resolution: Run dry-run, show impact, require approval, and keep rollback visible.

escalation.minimum_required_human_decision: Choose evidence to attach, or approve a scoped deferral.
escalation.reason: Required evidence is missing for a durable claim or action.
escalation.recommended_resolution: Attach an Evidence Bundle or narrow the mission.

escalation.minimum_required_human_decision: Pick the acceptable interpretation or request more evidence.
escalation.reason: Model or judge outputs disagree on a material mission result.
escalation.recommended_resolution: Use evidence-weighted adjudication and preserve dissent.

escalation.minimum_required_human_decision: Decide whether to revise the request or keep it denied.
escalation.reason: Policy denied the requested mission/action step.
escalation.recommended_resolution: Review the policy reason and adjust scope, risk, or authority.

escalation.minimum_required_human_decision: Provide the minimum goal clarification needed to continue.
escalation.reason: Mission goal is ambiguous enough to risk wrong action.
escalation.recommended_resolution: Clarify goal, success criteria, and stop conditions.

CornerStone is the intelligence, evidence, mission, action-control, and learning layer over them.
Connected sources are accessed through governed capabilities and action approvals.

walkthrough.boundary_explanation: Internal engines are implementation boundaries; daily users see one CornerStone workflow.
capability_language: Use governed action cards for safe action.
Keep audit and learning visible as product surfaces.

action_reversibility.non_reversible_explanation: External system semantics may prevent reversal; owner must receive impact warning before execution.
